name: openrouter_models_pricing_scraper
title: OpenRouter Models Pricing Scraper
description: Fetch and filter the public OpenRouter model catalog, returning normalized model pricing, context windows, modalities, tokenizer families, provider limits, supported parameters, Hugging Face IDs, and OpenRouter model URLs.

seo.title: OpenRouter Models Pricing Scraper Tool for LLM Catalog and Cost Data
seo.description: Fetch the public OpenRouter model catalog with Better Fetch, including model IDs, providers, modalities, context length, token pricing, cache pricing, web search pricing, supported parameters, and model URLs.
seo.intro: OpenRouter Models Pricing Scraper gives agents and product teams a structured snapshot of OpenRouter's public LLM catalog. Pull the catalog in one bounded request, filter by provider, modality, context length, free-only status, prompt price, completion price, or keyword, then return normalized rows with model IDs, canonical slugs, names, descriptions, providers, OpenRouter URLs, context windows, max completion tokens, modalities, tokenizer families, USD-per-million prompt and completion prices, cache and image pricing when published, moderation flags, Hugging Face IDs, supported parameters, reasoning labels, and details URLs. It is designed for AI gateway catalogs, cost-aware model routing, FinOps dashboards, procurement snapshots, model comparison pages, and agent-readable LLM capability inventories.

useCases.title: Cost-aware routing
useCases.description: Filter OpenRouter models by context length, modality, and price ceiling before choosing candidates for an AI routing layer.

useCases.title: LLM FinOps dashboards
useCases.description: Normalize prompt and completion prices to USD per million tokens so finance and engineering teams can compare models quickly.

useCases.title: Model catalog enrichment
useCases.description: Add provider, tokenizer, supported-parameter, modality, context-window, Hugging Face, and OpenRouter URL fields to internal AI catalogs.

faqs.question: Does OpenRouter Models Pricing Scraper require an OpenRouter API key?
faqs.answer: No. Version 0.1 reads OpenRouter's public model catalog endpoint. You only need an OpenRouter key when you want to call the models themselves.

faqs.question: Does it call or benchmark the models?
faqs.answer: No. It only reads catalog metadata and pricing. It does not send prompts, measure latency, or consume OpenRouter model credits.

faqs.question: How is pricing normalized?
faqs.answer: OpenRouter publishes token-level prices. The tool multiplies token prices by 1,000,000 and returns USD-per-million-token fields for easier comparison.

query.description: Optional free-text search across model id, name, description, and Hugging Face ID.

provider.description: Optional OpenRouter model id prefix such as anthropic, openai, google, meta-llama, mistralai, or qwen.

modality.description: Optional exact modality such as text->text or text+image->text.

max_prompt_price_per_million.description: Maximum prompt price in USD per 1M tokens.

max_completion_price_per_million.description: Maximum completion price in USD per 1M tokens.

free_only.description: Only return models with zero prompt and completion prices.

total_available.description: Total model rows in the source catalog before filters

prompt_price_per_million.description: Prompt price in USD per 1M tokens

completion_price_per_million.description: Completion price in USD per 1M tokens

input_cache_read_price_per_million.description: Input cache read price in USD per 1M tokens

input_cache_write_price_per_million.description: Input cache write price in USD per 1M tokens

image_price_per_million.description: Image price in USD per 1M units when published

web_search_price.description: Web search unit price when published

internal_reasoning_price_per_million.description: Internal reasoning price in USD per 1M tokens

hugging_face_id.description: Hugging Face model ID when published
